{{Quote|I have leaned on Jan in far more dangerous instances than these.|Terrinald Screed, receiving the Holt Cross alongside Jan Dodonna for their part in the Battle of Anaxes|<ref name="Warfare" />}}
The '''Battle of Anaxes''' of [[21 BBY/Legends|20.1 BBY]] was the final confrontation between the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]' [[Bulwark Fleet]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Victory Fleet]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. After [[Battle of Foerost (Clone Wars)|launching]] the Bulwark Fleet from the [[Foerost Shipyards]], [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Dua Ningo/Legends|Dua Ningo]] initiated an [[Foerost campaign|intense campaign]] against the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], coming close to seizing [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] itself. The Victory Fleet's [[Commander/Legends|commanders]], [[Captain/Legends|Captains]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]], hunted down Ningo and [[Space warfare/Legends|engaged]] his [[Bulwark Mark I]] [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruisers]] in a deciding clash over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]]. Although Screed outmaneuvered and vaporized Ningo's [[Unrepentant (Bulwark Mark I)|flagship]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] the Separatist admiral, Screed's assault cost him damage to his own [[Arlionne|command ship]], resulting in near-mortal injuries that forced him to undergo [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] reconstruction. Nevertheless, he and Dodonna both received the [[Holt Cross]] for their victorious actions over Anaxes.

==Prelude==
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[21 BBY/Legends|20.1 BBY]] ([[Great ReSynchronization|15:4]]), twenty-three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] unveiled its new [[Bulwark Fleet]] of [[Bulwark Mark I]] [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruisers]].<ref name="NEC" /> [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]]<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Dua Ningo/Legends|Dua Ningo]] oversaw the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Battle of Foerost (Clone Wars)|breakout]] from the [[Foerost Shipyards]] and proceeded to [[Foerost campaign|obliterate]] [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] outposts throughout the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], threatening the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] itself. In response, two [[Republic officer/Legends|Republic officers]]—[[Captain/Legends|Captains]] [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]] and [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]]—were granted command of the latest addition to the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]]: the [[Victory Fleet]] of [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyers]],<ref name="NEC" /> designed by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex]].<ref name="Warfare" />

[[File:Anaxes_space.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Anaxes, the site of the battle]]
Screed and Dodonna chased Ningo away from Coruscant<ref name="CSWE Ningo" /> and pursued him throughout the Core Worlds. The Victory and Bulwark Fleets clashed at [[Battle of Ixtlar (Clone Wars)|Ixtlar]], [[Battle of Alsakan (Clone Wars)|Alsakan]], and [[Battle of Basilisk (Clone Wars)|Basilisk]] before a deciding standoff ensued at<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] of [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]],<ref name="Coruscant and the Core Worlds">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> home to the [[Republic Navy War College/Legends|Republic Navy War College]].<ref name="Warfare" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|The final showdown came in the skies above Anaxes. Dodonna, under heavy fire with dead wrecks from his task force plunging planetward, held out long enough for Screed to pop in from hyperspace and disintegrate Ningo's flagship with a broadside fusillade.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
The Victory Fleet engaged in a deception: Dodonna arrived first at Anaxes with his task force of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and [[Space warfare/Legends|assaulted]] Ningo's Bulwark [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] in the upper [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. Screed, meanwhile, sat a mere [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump away<ref name="NEC" /> aboard his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]], the ''[[Arlionne]]'',<ref name="Warfare" /> waiting for the right moment to join the battle.<ref name="NEC" /> Commanding the Bulwark Fleet from his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Unrepentant (Bulwark Mark I)|''Unrepentant'']],<ref name="Warfare" /> Ningo decimated Dodonna's [[Warship/Legends|warships]], plunging dead wreck after dead wreck toward the surface below. Despite the carnage, Dodonna held out against Ningo's [[Starship/Legends|ships]] long enough for Screed to execute his sneak attack.<ref name="NEC" /> Emerging from hyperspace aboard the ''Arlionne'', Screed maneuvered his Star Destroyer into position and ambushed the ''Unrepentant'' at point-blank range. His swift salvo of broadside cannonades annihilated Ningo's flagship,<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Death/Legends|killing]] the Confederate admiral.<ref name="CSWE Ningo" /> However, the ensuing explosion of the ''Unrepentant'' tore into the ''Arlionne'', ripping open its [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and severely wounding Screed.<ref name="Warfare" /> Nevertheless, Ningo's death allowed Screed and Dodonna to emerge victorious over the late admiral's Bulwark Fleet.<ref name="NEC" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Screed required cybernetic reconstruction to survive the injuries he received in the Battle of Anaxes, but both men returned to Coruscant as heroes.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
[[File:Screed_and_Dodonna.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Screed and Dodonna are presented with the Holt Cross.]]
Screed needed [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implants to survive his near-mortal injuries,<ref name="NEC" /> but he began to recover and—within three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] of the Battle of Anaxes, as it came to be known—laboriously returned to Anaxes to receive the [[Holt Cross]] medal alongside Dodonna, in recognition of their defeat of the Bulwark Fleet.<ref name="Warfare" /> Screed and Dodonna were lauded as heroes of the Republic,<ref name="NEC" /> and both [[Gender/Legends|men]] continued their careers in the Republic [[Navy/Legends|Navy]]. Screed was thereafter appointed as the commander of the [[Coruscant Defense Fleet/Legends|Coruscant Home Fleet]], in part to grant him further recovery [[Time/Legends|time]] to heal from his wounds.<ref name="Warfare" />

In [[36 ABY]], [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] included the Battle of Anaxes in his [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|compendium of galactic history]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Anaxes was created by author [[Daniel Wallace]] for ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', a book published in [[2005]] that also served as an [[in-universe]] narrative written by Voren Na'al.<ref name="NEC" /> Among the research that he did for the book, Wallace drew upon the concept of the ''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer from the [[1979]] novel ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', using the Battle of Anaxes and the surrounding events to chronicle the Star Destroyer's formal in-universe introduction.<ref name="NEC Endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2005/10/endnotes-for-star-wars-new-essential.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The New Essential Chronology}}</ref> The battle has since been referenced and expanded by other authors, including [[Jason Fry]], who co-authored [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' and detailed the battle's events in a profile for Terrinald Screed.<ref name="Warfare" /><ref name="Warfare Endnotes 7">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/23039847571/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-7|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 7|quote=I wanted to give Screed a backstory that made him sympathetic and even sad, instead of being (literally) a cartoon villain. You can overdo this sort of thing—some would grouse that the EU already has—but I think letting Dodonna sum up Screed gives the portrayal a little detachment and keeps it from being mawkish.}}</ref>
